sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Pelvic binders are used when significant blunt trauma raises concern for an unstable pelvic ring injury, particularly when pain, deformity, haemodynamic compromise, or high-risk mechanism is present. Early application may reduce pelvic movement and help limit haemorrhage.

sections.title: Application (SAM Pelvic Sling / Commercial Binder)
sections.content:
1. Suspect unstable pelvic injury from mechanism, pain, deformity, or physiology rather than repeated pelvic rocking

2. Position the binder centrally over the greater trochanters, not the iliac crests

3. Slide or place the binder with minimal movement of the patient

4. Apply the device according to its design until the pelvis is firmly contained without excessive over-tightening

5. Secure the device and reassess pain, haemodynamic state, distal limb status, and overall packaging

6. Avoid unnecessary movement after application and coordinate subsequent transfers carefully

**Improvised sheet binder:**
- Fold a sheet into a broad band, position it over the greater trochanters, tighten firmly, and secure it in place

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Correct placement over the greater trochanters matters more than excessive tension
- Do not delay binder application in a strongly suspected unstable pelvic injury while waiting for hypotension to develop
- Avoid repeated pelvic assessment once the suspicion is established
- Document the indication, time applied, and patient response
